summary: The reproduction bundle builds the Jervis library from source and exercises the GroovyShell.evaluate() code injection path by loading a crafted .jervis.yml whose matrix exclude value contains a single-quote breakout that injects arbitrary Groovy code into the evaluated filter expression.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the injected Groovy expression to execute inside the container, writing a proof file to /tmp/evohunt_proof with the string EVOHUNT_CODE_EXECUTION_PROOF.
actual_observed_result: Not yet executed; the reproduction bundle was generated to be self-contained for future reruns.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks for the existence and content of /tmp/evohunt_proof after the exploit trigger runs. It distinguishes successful code injection from a normal filter evaluation by verifying the proof string matches exactly.
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction requires network access for Gradle dependency resolution during the build step.
The Gradle build may take several minutes depending on available bandwidth and cache state.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
